title: Baked Rice with Cinnamon and Shallots
description: Long grain brown rice baked in the oven with caramelized shallots, caraway seeds, cinnamon, and star anise, finished with toasted almonds, mint, and lemon zest. Minimal effort, maximum flavor.

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C fan). Peel and halve the shallots, then slice each half into thin strips lengthways. Transfer to a deep ovenproof dish, drizzle generously with olive oil and sea salt, and bake for 15 minutes.
Toast caraway seeds in a dry frying pan over gentle heat for a couple of minutes until they smell aromatic. Set aside.
Add rice to the dish with the shallots and caraway seeds and stir to combine. Pour over the warm stock, add butter, cinnamon stick, and star anise. Cover tightly with foil, ensuring no air escapes.
Bake for 35 minutes. Remove from oven and leave covered to rest 5 minutes.

Remove foil, discard cinnamon stick and star anise, and fluff rice with a fork. Season to taste. Transfer to a serving bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and top with almonds, mint leaves, and fresh lemon zest.

notes:
Baking rice rather than cooking it on the stovetop infuses far more flavor and requires almost no attention -- ideal for entertaining.

Warm the stock before adding -- cold stock will throw off the bake time.

If banana shallots aren't available, regular shallots work fine. Use 5-6 of them.
